mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in high PAYEMS periods fall below 0.5% in 60%+ of cases, or the correlation between PAYEMS and returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weak demand for construction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as economic activity contracts.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in low HOUST periods exceed 1.5% in 50%+ of cases, or the negative correlation between HOUST and returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong supply-side capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high INDPRO periods fall below 0.8% in 50%+ of cases, or the correlation between INDPRO and returns drops below 0.20.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% triggers central bank tightening expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high CPIAUCSL periods exceed 1.0% in 60%+ of cases, or the negative correlation between CPIAUCSL and returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in GDP (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate weakening demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in combined GDPC1/RSAFS decline periods exceed 1.2% in 50%+ of cases, or the interaction effect between GDPC1 and RSAFS on returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in unemployment (UNRATE) below 6% signals tight labor market conditions that drive equity valuations higher, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in low UNRATE periods fall below 0.3% in 50%+ of cases, or the positive correlation between UNRATE and returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising capacity utilization (not directly observed) signals strong supply-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high INDPRO periods fall below 0.7% in 50%+ of cases, or the correlation between INDPRO and returns drops below 0.15.
